radar.title: Technical Skills Radar
radar.subtitle: Mapped against the job description requirements
redFlagItems: No quantified impact on any bullet

issues.label: Pinned repos have no README
issues.detail: 3 pinned repositories lack documentation. Strong negative signal for senior roles.

issues.label: No CI/CD configuration detected
issues.detail: None of your repos include GitHub Actions or similar pipelines.

issues.label: Commit history gaps > 6 months
issues.detail: 3 visible gaps suggest inconsistent activity over the last 2 years.

issues.label: Zero recommendations received
issues.detail: No peer or manager recommendations. At senior level, social proof matters.

issues.label: Current role title too vague
issues.detail: "Developer" without seniority or domain signals. Recruiters can't assess fit at a glance.

issues.label: Summary section missing
issues.detail: Profile has no About section. You're losing the most prominent keyword-matching surface.

atsCard.label: ATS Filter
atsCard.title: Simulate the filter before the recruiter does
atsCard.desc: We run your CV through an ATS simulation matched to the job description. Each missing keyword gets a point value, so you know exactly what to add.

rewriteCard.label: Improve CV
rewriteCard.title: Your CV, surgically rewritten
rewriteCard.desc: One click rewrites the entire CV: keywords injected, passive tone converted, seniority signals added. Export a clean PDF.
rewriteCard.beforeText: "Responsible for backend development and worked on API features for the platform."
rewriteCard.afterText: Led backend architecture delivering 4 core REST APIs, reducing P95 latency by 40% across 200K daily requests.

interviewCard.label: AI Interview
interviewCard.title: 10-minute voice mock interview
interviewCard.desc: A real-time AI interviewer, voice-based and role-specific. Ends with a scored debrief across communication, technical depth, and leadership signals.
interviewCard.aiQuestion: "Walk me through a system you designed from scratch. What trade-offs did you make?"
interviewCard.youAnswer: "I built a real-time notification service handling 50K events/sec..."

bridge.projectDesc: Build a production-ready microservices system using Docker, Kubernetes, and an event-driven architecture via Kafka. Exposes real-world CI/CD pipeline experience and closes the DevOps gap identified in your radar.
bridge.architectureText: API Gateway → Auth Service → Order Service → Kafka → Notification Service + Inventory Service. Each service containerised with Docker, orchestrated via Kubernetes, deployed via GitHub Actions.
whatToBuild: API Gateway with JWT auth, rate limiting, and request routing
Kafka producer/consumer for async inter-service communication
CI/CD pipeline with automated Docker builds and K8s deployments
Distributed tracing with OpenTelemetry and Prometheus metrics
Zero-downtime rolling deploy strategy with health checks

coverLetter.headerLabel: Cover Letter · Senior Backend Engineer @ Stripe
coverLetter.p1: I'm applying for the Senior Backend Engineer role with a focus on distributed systems, an area where I've spent the last four years building high-throughput, event-driven infrastructure. At my current company, I led the redesign of our payments pipeline, reducing P99 latency from 340ms to 62ms across 800K daily transactions.
coverLetter.p2: What draws me to Stripe is the engineering culture around reliability and API design. Your recent engineering blog post on idempotency keys resonated with a problem I solved last year when we migrated from a monolith to microservices, ensuring exactly-once delivery across a Kafka-backed system with 15 consumers.
coverLetter.p3: I'd welcome the opportunity to discuss how my experience with...

trust.title: Why users trust RejectCheck
trust.subtitle: No manufactured claims. Every fact below is verifiable by reading the privacy policy or the product itself.

items.label: Job-specific, not generic
items.claim: Every analysis is anchored to a real job description, not a generic CV scan. RejectCheck measures keyword gaps, seniority fit, tone match, and required skills against the actual posting you're applying to. A CV that scores 90% on one role may score 50% on another.
items.source: Observable by running two analyses with different job descriptions on the same CV

items.label: Scored diagnosis in about a minute
items.claim: The scored diagnosis streams in around a minute on a typical CV + job description pair; the deep pass (per-issue fixes, project recommendation, ATS keywords) is then generated and delivered right after.
items.source: Observable by timing any analysis on /en/analyze

items.label: Bilingual by design
items.claim: English and French are both first-class: UI, analysis output, keyword detection, tone audit, and FAQ are fully localized, not machine-translated as an afterthought. Jobscan, Rezi, Resume Worded, and Enhancv are English-only as of May 2026.

items.label: Free tier is real
items.claim: Guest users get 1 full analysis without signup or credit card, not a teaser. Registered (free) users get 3 analyses. Premium sections are blurred, not absent: the full analysis runs and is saved, only gated views are hidden until upgrade.
items.source: Verifiable by running a guest analysis and inspecting the result

items.label: Guest data auto-deleted in 7 days
items.claim: For guest scans, your CV, job description, and result are automatically deleted from our database within 7 days — after that only your IP and a timestamp remain, to enforce the 1-analysis-per-guest limit. Registered users' analyses are kept in their dashboard until they choose to delete them, and no data is ever used to train AI models.
items.source: Privacy policy, section on data handling

items.label: Hired plan refund is genuine
items.claim: If you subscribe to Hired (€39.99/month) and get hired during an active billing month, we refund that month on receipt of an accepted offer letter. This is a contractual guarantee clause, not marketing language.
items.source: Refund policy documented in the pricing FAQ

s01.subtitle: You send 80 applications. 78 disappear into silence. RejectCheck reads your CV the way a recruiter would — and tells you, before you apply, exactly what would have killed it.
s01.dropHint: PDF or DOCX up to 5MB · no signup required for the first run

s02.h2Part1: Six dimensions a recruiter scans in
s02.h2Part2: We score each one, in numbers.

items.title: ATS
items.desc: We simulate the 4 most-used Applicant Tracking Systems and tell you exactly which required terms are missing — including singular/plural and synonym variants.

items.title: Seniority
items.desc: "5+ years" vs "8 years" reads very differently. We map your titles, scope, and tenure against the band the JD expects.

items.title: Quantified
items.desc: Bullets without numbers blur. We flag every bullet missing a metric and suggest where to add one — truthfully.

items.title: Red
items.desc: Unexplained gaps, vague titles, inconsistencies. The things a recruiter spots in two seconds — we surface them before they do.

items.title: GitHub /
items.desc: For technical roles, your GitHub matters more than your bullets. We read public activity, language mix, and project depth, then weigh them against the JD.

items.title: Tone &
items.desc: Buzzword density, voice consistency, length-to-impact ratio. We tell you where it sounds like a template — and where it sounds like you.

s03.subtitle: The report you get isn't a thumbs-up or down. It's an actionable document — the kind you'd pay a senior recruiter $300 to write for you.
s03.topFixTitle: The one fix that moves you most
s03.topFixBody: Add distributed systems, gRPC, and observability to your bullets where they truthfully apply. Estimated score impact: −22 points.

compRows.sub: Tells you when you'll be rejected, not just "matched".
Reads your public repos and weighs them against the JD.
Your CV, LinkedIn and GitHub reconciled against one job — not a single keyword scan.
Suggests truthful rewrites, not generic action verbs.
Score, keywords, and red flags are always free.
Premium analysis, billed monthly. Cancel anytime.

s05.founderQuote: During my job search I built the tool I wish existed, this same tool got me 4x more interviews and a job in 2 months

s07.quotePart1: The rejection arrives in silence. Or it doesn't arrive at all.
s07.quoteRed: We just thought you'd want to know first.

footerBrand.tagline: Tells you why you'd be rejected — before you apply. Built for developers, works for any role.

whatsNew.title: Beyond one-shot CV analysis
whatsNew.subtitle: The CV diagnosis is the core, but RejectCheck now includes daily code review drills, team licensing for coaches and bootcamps, and honest comparisons to every major CV tool.

items.label: Daily code review challenge
items.description: One real production code snippet, AI-reviewed every day. Build your senior-engineer pattern-matching muscles with a 5-minute daily drill. Streaks, badges, scored debrief.

items.label: RejectCheck for Teams
items.description: Bulk licensing for career coaches, coding bootcamps, university career centers, and outplacement firms. Volume pricing from 10 seats, contact us for a quote. Early-access, manual onboarding under 24h.

items.label: Compare RejectCheck vs the competition
items.description: Hands-on honest comparisons vs Jobscan, Rezi, Resume Worded, Kickresume, and Enhancv. Published pricing, honest weaknesses, no fluff. Pick the tool that fits your situation.

faq.title: Pricing FAQ
faq.subtitle: Cancellation, refunds, payment methods, plan changes.

items.question: Can I cancel my RejectCheck subscription anytime?
items.answer: Yes. You can cancel your Shortlisted (€19.99/month) or Hired (€39.99/month) subscription at any time from the account settings page. You keep full access until the end of the already-billed period - no prorated charges.

items.question: Is there a free trial or free tier?
items.answer: Yes. The Free plan is free forever and includes 1 deep CV analysis for guests (no signup) or 3 analyses for registered users, with ATS simulation, tone audit, seniority audit, and red-flag detection. No credit card required.

items.question: Does the Hired plan actually refund me if I get hired?
items.answer: Yes. If you get hired during your active Hired billing month, email support@rejectcheck.com with proof of offer (an accepted offer letter or signed contract) and we refund your current month. It is a genuine guarantee, not marketing language.

items.question: What payment methods does RejectCheck accept?
items.answer: Payments are processed by Stripe and accept all major credit and debit cards (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover) plus European SEPA direct debit and Apple Pay / Google Pay where supported by Stripe in your region.

items.question: Can I switch between Shortlisted and Hired plans?
items.answer: Yes. You can upgrade from Shortlisted to Hired or downgrade at any time from the account page. Upgrades are prorated immediately; downgrades take effect at the end of the current billing period.

items.question: How does RejectCheck compare to Jobscan, Rezi, and other alternatives?
items.answer: At €19.99/month, Shortlisted sits comfortably within the range of tools like Rezi ($29/month), Jobscan, and Enhancv — without being the most expensive. What sets RejectCheck apart isn't the price tag: it's the depth. You get a multi-pass Claude pipeline (Sonnet + Haiku) with a full GitHub/LinkedIn cross-check, the kind of analysis the others don't run at any tier. Pay for the analysis, not the brand.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about RejectCheck before you upload your CV.

items.question: What is RejectCheck?
items.answer: RejectCheck is an AI-powered CV and job application diagnosis tool. In about a minute, it shows you exactly why your application is getting filtered - running a multi-pass Claude pipeline (a Sonnet diagnostic for ATS fit, red flags and skill-gap radar, plus a Haiku pass that cross-references your GitHub and LinkedIn) against the specific job description.

items.question: How does RejectCheck analyze my CV?
items.answer: Upload your CV and paste the target job description. RejectCheck runs a multi-pass Claude pipeline: a fast Sonnet diagnostic (ATS keyword/format check, tone audit, red-flag detection, technical skill-gap radar) plus a Haiku pass that cross-references your CV, LinkedIn and GitHub. The scored diagnosis arrives in about a minute, with the deep fixes generated right after.

items.question: What is an ATS and how does RejectCheck simulate it?
items.answer: An Applicant Tracking System (ATS) is the software recruiters use to store and rank applications. Contrary to a popular myth, most ATS don't auto-reject you on a keyword score — a human recruiter still makes the call, usually in a 6-second scan. So RejectCheck does both: it checks the machine-readable layer (parsing + the keywords weighted by the job description) AND tells you why a human would set your CV aside. We show you the real reasons, not a magic pass-rate.

items.question: Is RejectCheck free?
items.answer: Yes. The Free plan is free forever: 1 deep analysis (no signup) or 3 analyses for registered users, with ATS simulation, tone audit, red-flag detection, and CV score. Premium plans (€19.99 or €39.99 per month) unlock 15 or 30 full analyses per month, CV rewrite, and extra runs you can buy anytime that never expire.

items.question: Does RejectCheck audit my GitHub and LinkedIn profiles?
items.answer: Yes. Add your GitHub username and a LinkedIn PDF export at analysis time. Claude audits commit history, repo quality, language distribution, and project relevance against the target role, and cross-references your LinkedIn profile (titles, summary, recommendations) with your CV to catch inconsistencies.

items.question: Which AI models does RejectCheck use?
items.answer: RejectCheck runs on Anthropic Claude — Sonnet for the diagnostic (ATS keyword/format check, CV audit, red-flag detection, technical skill-gap radar) and Haiku for the multi-source profile digest (GitHub/LinkedIn cross-reference) — plus Google Gemini for optional starter-repo generation. The privacy policy documents which provider processes which data.

items.question: How is RejectCheck different from other CV tools?
items.answer: Most CV tools sell a magic "ATS score" and stop at a generic keyword list. RejectCheck judges your rejection risk against the specific job description, visualizes skill gaps on a radar chart, audits GitHub and LinkedIn as extra signals, recommends a concrete project (with a generated starter repo) to close real gaps, and tells you why a human recruiter would set your application aside. It is bilingual (English and French).

meta.title: For Teams - Bootcamps & Coaches
meta.description: Bulk licensing of AI CV diagnosis for career coaches, bootcamps, university career centers, and outplacement firms. Volume pricing.

hero.badge: For career coaches · bootcamps · universities · outplacement
hero.title: RejectCheck for teams serving job seekers
hero.subtitle: Help your clients, students, or candidates land interviews faster. One multi-pass Claude CV diagnosis pipeline (Sonnet + Haiku), bulk licensing, and custom pricing for teams of 10 to 10,000.
hero.availabilityNote: Currently early-access for teams. Individual plans shipping now; team infrastructure provisioned manually by our team on request - no self-serve team dashboard yet.

segments.title: Four teams already using tools like this

items.label: Career coaches
items.text: Manage 10-50 clients? Show them an objective ATS score, technical skill gap radar, and GitHub audit - not just your opinion. Your coaching becomes data-driven. Volume pricing from 10 seats.

items.label: Coding bootcamps
items.text: Graduate better candidates. Give your students unlimited CV diagnoses during career week so your career managers focus on high-value coaching, not repetitive reviews. Bulk licensing for 30-500 seats per cohort.

items.label: University career centers
items.text: Self-service CV diagnosis for your entire student body. Advisors handle high-value conversations; students iterate on their own. Volume licensing for 1,000+ seats with custom terms.

items.label: Outplacement & recruiting firms
items.text: Equip your career transition consultants with a data-driven CV tool. Per-consultant licensing. White-label PDF reports available on request for enterprise contracts.

howItWorks.title: Three steps, no self-serve dashboard required

steps.title: Contact us with your use case
steps.text: Email business@rejectcheck.com or book a 15-min call. Tell us your segment (coach, bootcamp, university, outplacement), expected number of seats, and billing preference.

steps.title: We onboard your team within 24h
steps.text: We provision the accounts for your clients/students, send a custom Stripe invoice (PO-friendly), and share onboarding guides for your users. No CSV import needed yet - we do it for you.

steps.title: Your users run unlimited analyses
steps.text: Each seat gets the full RejectCheck experience: ATS check, skill gap radar, GitHub/LinkedIn audit, red-flag detection, and CV rewrite. Same features as individual Hired-tier users.

status.title: What's available today vs. on the roadmap
availableToday: Monthly or annual Stripe invoicing (PO numbers supported)
Full multi-pass Claude diagnosis per user (Sonnet + Haiku)
status.roadmapLabel: On the roadmap (building as demand grows)
roadmap: SSO - Google Workspace, Okta, Azure AD
White-label PDF reports with your branding

pricing.title: Volume pricing - contact us for a quote
pricing.subtitle: No self-serve team purchase yet. We quote based on seats, term length, and segment. Typical deals close within 3 business days.

tiers.name: Team
features: Best for career coaches and small consulting firms
Same analysis features as individual Hired tier

tiers.name: Bootcamp / University
features: Best for coding bootcamps and university career centers

tiers.name: Enterprise
features: Best for outplacement firms, large universities, multinational HR
White-label and SSO as custom integration

items.question: How are team accounts provisioned today?
items.answer: Manually, by our team, within 24 hours of your first invoice payment. We create each user account in our Supabase Auth backend and email the credentials to you or your users directly. No team dashboard or bulk CSV import is live yet - that's on the roadmap.

items.question: Can I get a custom invoice with a PO number for accounting?
items.answer: Yes. Stripe supports custom invoices with PO numbers, VAT fields, and custom memo. Tell us your accounting requirements at the onboarding call and we handle it. Net-30 terms available for annual contracts on the Bootcamp/University and Enterprise tiers.

items.question: Do you support SSO (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace)?
items.answer: Not as a standard feature yet. SSO is on the roadmap and available as a custom integration for Enterprise contracts - quote includes the engineering work. For Team and Bootcamp/University tiers, users sign in with email + password like individual users.

items.question: Can I cancel monthly team licenses at any time?
items.answer: Yes, same terms as individual plans. Cancel anytime from the billing contact; access continues until the end of the already-paid period. For annual contracts, cancellation is available at renewal. We do not lock teams into multi-year minimums.

items.question: Do team members get the same features as individual Hired-tier users?
items.answer: Yes - full diagnosis including GitHub audit, LinkedIn cross-reference, technical skill gap radar, red-flag detection, and CV rewrite. The only thing that differs is billing (you pay, not them) and account provisioning (we create accounts, not self-signup).

items.question: Can we white-label the PDF exports with our logo?
items.answer: Not as a standard feature yet. Available today as custom engineering work for Enterprise contracts. On the roadmap as a standard feature - we'll build it once we have 3+ teams requesting it.

items.question: How do you handle candidate data privacy?
items.answer: Per our privacy policy, CV data is processed by Anthropic Claude (and Google Gemini for optional starter-repo generation) APIs for analysis. For team deployments, we sign custom DPAs on request. Candidate-facing users control their own data (they can delete anytime). For universities and enterprise contracts, GDPR-compliant processing is documented and auditable.

cta.title: Ready to talk?
cta.subtitle: Email us your use case and expected seat count - we reply within 24 hours with a custom quote.

step1.title: First,
step1.sub: Pick the closest one. We tune the rejection-risk model differently for every role, so this matters.
step1.otherLabel: Tell us in your own words

step2.title: Where are you
step2.sub: Junior CVs and Senior CVs get rejected for completely different reasons. This tells the model which red flags to look for.

step3.title: Pick your
step3.sub: What you'd actually put in the top half of your CV. Not stuff you "touched once at uni." Three is the cap on purpose.

step4.title: Languages you speak,
step4.sub: A junior dev fluent in German is a totally different match for a Berlin role. Pick at least one.
step4.searchPlaceholder: Search a language… (English, Français, 中文)

step5.eyebrow: Last bit · Where to find you
step5.title: Drop a link or two.
step5.sub: We pull public commits, post history, and portfolio data to back up what your CV claims. Nothing private. Skip any field if you don't have it.

step6.title: Your radar is
step6.sub: We've tuned the model to your role, level, and stack. Time to put your CV through it. Most people are surprised by what comes back.

software.title: Tuned for software roles
software.text: GitHub is recommended. Your commit history is half the signal. LinkedIn and a portfolio site help, but GitHub is what hiring managers click first.

data.title: Tuned for data / ML roles
data.text: We pull notebooks, Kaggle profile, and dataset contributions from GitHub. LinkedIn fills in the credentials story.

product.title: Tuned for product roles
product.text: PM hiring leans on LinkedIn: recommendations, scope of work, company logos. A case-study portfolio is the differentiator at mid+ level.

design.title: Tuned for design roles
design.text: Portfolio is everything. We treat it as the primary artifact. LinkedIn is secondary signal for your career timeline.

marketing.title: Tuned for marketing / growth
marketing.text: LinkedIn for the network and history; portfolio (or a published-work doc) for the actual receipts: campaigns, posts, dashboards.

ops.title: Tuned for operations
ops.text: LinkedIn carries 80% of the signal here. We won't bug you for a portfolio you don't need.

sales.title: Tuned for sales / business
sales.text: LinkedIn does the heavy lifting: quota, tenure, and recommendations are what recruiters scan for.

other.title: Adaptive setup
other.text: Drop whatever's relevant. We'll figure out which signals matter once we see your CV.

cvReviewTab.qualitySubtitle: Six dimensions scored from 0–100. Overall is a weighted average.
cvReviewTab.profileSubtitle: How a recruiter reading your CV blind would classify your profile.
cvReviewTab.atsSubtitle: Format and structure issues that may cause ATS rejection before a human reads your CV.
cvReviewTab.toneSubtitle: Passive writing weakens the recruiter's perception of your impact. Rewrite these with action verbs.
cvReviewTab.passiveExamples: Passive phrases found in your CV — rewrite each with a strong action verb

parsedCv.summary: ▸ See what an ATS reads from your PDF
parsedCv.hint: This is the plain text an ATS extracts from your file. If it looks garbled or out of order, the recruiter's software sees the same — fix your PDF's structure (avoid tables, columns, headers/footers, and images for text).

heroHeadline.below: You're below the line for this one.
heroHeadline.competitive: Competitive — with a few gaps to close.

report.positioningSub: Before a single bullet is read, two things set the first impression: the level you project, and the voice you write in.
report.auditTitle: Every issue, ranked by what it costs you
report.auditSub: issues across the document. Each opens with the fix and the time it takes.
report.redFlagsSub: patterns a reviewer spots in the first 8 seconds — before reading a single bullet.
report.requirementsSub: Every required skill from the job description, checked against what your CV actually demonstrates.

deepLocked.desc: How to fix each issue + your bridge project + the missing ATS keywords. Generated on demand.

hero.confidenceTooltip: How certain the model is in this analysis. Higher when signals from multiple sources align; lower when data is thin or contradictory.

match.title: Where you cover the role — and where you don't
match.subtitle: The filled shape is you; the dashed outline is the role's target. The ranked list shows what to close first.
match.chartLabel: Skill coverage · you vs the role
match.priorityTitle: Job priority · skills ranked by gap

ats.cutoffTooltip: Scores below the cutoff are automatically filtered out by the ATS before a human ever sees your CV.
ats.simulatorSubtitle: Add these, watch the score climb
ats.simulatorDesc: Each is a keyword the JD weights and your CV is missing. Tick to preview the lift — it's reversible.

cv.positioningTitle: How your CV reads at a glance

flags.requirementsTitle: JD skills, matched to your CV

rewritePaywall.title: CV rewrite & cover letter
rewritePaywall.desc: Paste-ready rewrites based on exactly what this scan found. Passive bullets made active, ATS keywords injected, seniority signals added.
rewritePaywall.unlockNote: One-time · this CV only · no subscription · up to 5 rewrites

plan.negotiationPremium: Negotiation analysis available on the Hired plan.

cv.title: Your CV,
cv.subtitle: Surgical edits grounded in exactly what this scan flagged — your achievements, sharpened. Nothing invented.
cv.statPassiveDesc: Rewritten as active, quantified wins — "responsible for" becomes shipped, owned, cut.
cv.statAtsDesc: Woven naturally into your real experience. Zero keyword stuffing.
cv.statSeniorityDesc: Scope, ownership and impact added where the scan saw gaps.
cv.statAuditDesc: Structure and clarity corrected — only on the sections flagged.
cv.previewTitle: The exact edits, before and after
cv.previewSubtitle: Pulled straight from this analysis — these are your lines, reframed.
cv.keywordsNote: — placed where the parser expects them, in your own words
cv.trustDesc: Every change traces back to a specific issue found in this scan. Your voice stays intact.

coverLetter.title: Addresses the JD,
coverLetter.subtitle: Generated from your analysis — it leans on the requirements you already meet and frames the gaps honestly.

signalsTabNew.githubEmpty: No GitHub profile was provided for this analysis. Add your GitHub URL to get signal coverage.
signalsTabNew.linkedinEmpty: No LinkedIn profile was provided for this analysis. Add your LinkedIn URL to get signal coverage.
